summary: Multi-platform pricing feature verified end-to-end. Settings API persists shopeefood/gofood/grabfood fee fields and frontend exposes them. Menu dialog offline_price input drives platform_prices preview (3 cards) using fees from settings. Math validated: offline=25000 with fees 20/22/22 yields ShopeeFood=31500 (net 25200), GoFood=32500 (net 25350), GrabFood=32500 (net 25350). Fallback to recommended_price when offline_price=0 also works.
passed: GET /api/settings returns platform fee fields (after first save)
POST /api/menus/preview-hpp with offline_price=25000 returns platform_prices [3] with correct rounding (31500/32500/32500)
POST /api/menus persists offline_price and returns computed.platform_prices
Fallback: when offline_price=0, computed.offline_price falls back to recommended_price (31500) and platform_prices computed from it
Settings UI: shopeefood-fee-input, gofood-fee-input, grabfood-fee-input visible and bound to backend values
Menu dialog: menu-offline-price-input visible; entering 25000 renders 3 cards platform-price-shopeefood/gofood/grabfood with price, fee%, net received, margin
notes: Settings DB currently holds shopeefood=65, gofood=38, grabfood=20 (leftover from prior tests); frontend cards reflect these — math still correct (25000/(1-0.65)=71500 rounded up). Defaults (20/22/22) only apply if fields absent from Pydantic model loading; confirmed by direct preview-hpp request which uses Settings defaults consistently.
